sections.title: What is the Present Simple?
sections.content:
We use the **Present Simple** for things that are always true, or things we do often.

• I **work** in a shop.
• She **likes** coffee.
• They **live** in London.

sections.title: Form: I, you, we, they
sections.content:
With **I, you, we, they** we use the verb as it is:

• I **work**
• You **work**
• We **work**
• They **work**

sections.title: Form: he, she, it
sections.content:
With **he, she, it** we add **-s** or **-es**:

• He **works**
• She **works**
• It **works**

• I **go** → She **goes**
• I **watch** → He **watches**

sections.title: Negatives
sections.content:
**I, you, we, they** + **don't** + verb:
• I **don't like** coffee.

**He, she, it** + **doesn't** + verb:
• She **doesn't work** on Saturdays.

sections.title: Questions
sections.content:
**Do** + I/you/we/they + verb?
• **Do you like** coffee?

**Does** + he/she/it + verb?
• **Does she work** here?

**Do** and **Does** go at the start. The verb is always the base form (no -s).
